title: App Page (`app/page.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the main landing page for the DocMed application. It utilizes Next.js App Router conventions and incorporates various UI components and animations to create an engaging user experience.

## Purpose

The `app/page.tsx` file serves as the entry point for the application's home page. It orchestrates the display of different sections, including a navigation bar, hero section with an interactive globe, trust bar, feature highlights, a "how it works" explanation, statistics, a call-to-action form, and a footer.

## Key Components and Exports

-   **`Navbar`**: A fixed navigation bar with responsive design, featuring a logo, navigation links, and user authentication buttons. It includes a mobile menu toggle.
-   **`Hero`**: The main hero section showcasing the "AI-Powered · Self-Healing" concept with a large, animated headline, descriptive text, and primary call-to-action buttons. It uses a `DotGlobeHero` component for a dynamic background.
-   **`TrustBar`**: A horizontal section displaying key attributes or technologies used by DocMed, such as "GitHub App", "Webhook-Driven", etc.
-   **`Features`**: A section detailing the core features of DocMed, each presented with an icon, title, description, and a highlighted benefit. It uses `motion` for staggered animations.
-   **`HowItWorks`**: An explanatory section that breaks down the DocMed workflow into three clear steps, each with an icon, title, and description.
-   **`Stats`**: A section presenting key metrics and statistics related to DocMed's effectiveness and ease of use.
-   **`CTA` (Call to Action)**: A prominent section encouraging users to sign up. It includes an email input form and submit button, along with persuasive copy.
-   **`Footer`**: The application's footer, containing the logo and copyright information.
-   **`Home` (Default Export)**: The main React component that renders all the above sections in sequence, forming the complete landing page.

## Animation and Styling

The page makes extensive use of `framer-motion` for animations like fading, sliding, and staggering element appearances. It also leverages Tailwind CSS for styling and responsiveness.

## Usage Example

This file is the main page and is automatically rendered by Next.js when a user navigates to the root URL (`/`). No explicit import or usage in other components is typically required for this file itself.

```jsx
// app/page.tsx

// ... imports and component definitions ...

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      <Features />
      <HowItWorks />
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```
